mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments that require efficient orchestration of large language models, balancing latency and throughput. Key trade-offs include complexity in setup and the need for adequate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the llm-d CLI tool to manage deployments and configurations.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI for Linux.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the required version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster credentials.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
Deploy llm-d using Helm to manage the installation and configuration.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Install llm-d in its own namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for llm-d.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command sets up autoscaling for your deployment.
> This command sets up monitoring for your deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
Remove llm-d and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data and configurations related to llm-d.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> This command uninstalls the llm-d release.
> This command deletes the llm-d namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is private.

**Fix:**
Ensure the image is available and accessible, or check your image pull secrets.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n llm-d' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU/Memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The nodes do not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or add more nodes to the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The request to the Kubernetes API server is timing out.

**Fix:**
Check network connectivity and ensure the API server is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for dynamic multi-workload autoscaling and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d'. All pods should be in 'Running' status.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1
helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
